assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response clearly states PDB blocking drain is the 'most common cause' and 'almost certainly' the issue, dedicating a full section to it as the primary diagnosis

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Provides 'kubectl get pdb -A -o wide' command which is equivalent to --all-namespaces

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Includes multiple commands: kubectl describe pdb to see protected pods, kubectl get pods | grep Pending for pending pods, and kubectl get events for FailedScheduling events

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Has dedicated section 'Check for resource constraints' with commands to check pending pods, FailedScheduling events, and node capacity using kubectl top nodes

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Provides kubectl patch command to temporarily adjust PDB with maxUnavailable 50% and includes backup/restore instructions

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Provides gcloud command to adjust max-surge-upgrade and max-unavailable-upgrade settings as a fix for resource constraints

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Webhook issues are not mentioned anywhere in the response as a potential cause of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Has dedicated 'Monitor Progress' section with watch commands for node status, pod movement, and upgrade operation status to validate fixes are working
